title: Academic Word List — Core
description: High-frequency general academic vocabulary in the spirit of the Academic Word List (AWL) — the process, reasoning, and argument words that recur across IELTS Reading, Writing, and academic texts.

cards.definition: to examine something in detail in order to understand or explain it
cards.example: The researchers analysed the survey data to identify emerging trends.

cards.definition: a particular way of dealing with a problem or task
cards.example: The study adopts a qualitative approach to examining classroom behaviour.

cards.definition: to judge or estimate the value, quality, or importance of something
cards.example: The report assesses the impact of tourism on local economies.

cards.definition: to accept that something is true without proof, as a basis for reasoning
cards.example: The model assumes that all participants act rationally.

cards.definition: an abstract idea or general notion
cards.example: The concept of sustainability underpins much of the current debate.

cards.definition: to be the parts that together form something; to make up
cards.example: Women constitute nearly half of the workforce in this sector.

cards.definition: the circumstances or setting in which something occurs and can be understood
cards.example: These findings must be interpreted within their historical context.

cards.definition: to help to cause or bring about a result
cards.example: Several factors contributed to the decline of the empire.

cards.definition: to state the exact meaning of a word or concept
cards.example: The author carefully defines each term before using it.

cards.definition: to obtain something from a source, or to develop from an origin
cards.example: Many English words are derived from Latin.

cards.definition: to share or spread something across an area or among a group
cards.example: Wealth is unevenly distributed across the region.

cards.definition: to set up something firmly, or to prove it beyond doubt
cards.example: The experiment established a clear link between the two variables.

cards.definition: to judge the value or effectiveness of something after careful consideration
cards.example: The committee evaluated each proposal against fixed criteria.

cards.definition: one of several things that influence or contribute to a result
cards.example: Cost was the decisive factor in their decision.

cards.definition: a basic structure of ideas used to plan or interpret something
cards.example: The theory provides a framework for analysing social change.

cards.definition: the natural purpose or role of something
cards.example: The primary function of the liver is to filter the blood.

cards.definition: to recognise and name something correctly
cards.example: The study identifies three main causes of the problem.

cards.definition: to show or suggest that something is true or exists
cards.example: The results indicate a strong correlation between the two variables.

cards.definition: to explain the meaning of something, or to decide how it should be understood
cards.example: The data can be interpreted in several different ways.

cards.definition: to include something as a necessary part or result
cards.example: The procedure involves several distinct stages.

cards.definition: a systematic way of doing something
cards.example: The researchers described their method in careful detail.

cards.definition: to happen or take place, especially without being planned
cards.example: The reaction occurs only at high temperatures.

cards.definition: a fundamental truth or rule that serves as the basis for reasoning
cards.example: The design is based on well-established scientific principles.

cards.definition: a series of actions or steps taken to achieve a result
cards.example: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants make their food.

cards.definition: to need something as necessary or essential
cards.example: This approach requires a large amount of reliable data.

cards.definition: large or important enough to have a noticeable effect
cards.example: There was a significant increase in productivity after the reform.

cards.definition: the way in which the parts of something are arranged or organised
cards.example: The essay has a clear and logical structure.

cards.definition: a system of ideas intended to explain something, based on general principles
cards.example: Darwin's theory of evolution transformed the study of biology.

cards.definition: to differ in size, amount, or degree from one case to another
cards.example: Results varied considerably between the two groups.

cards.definition: one of the parts that together make up a whole
cards.example: Trust is a key component of any successful team.

cards.definition: a result or effect of an action or condition
cards.example: Rising temperatures have serious consequences for agriculture.

cards.definition: to show clearly that something is true, especially by giving evidence
cards.example: The study demonstrates that early intervention improves outcomes.
